title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
summary: Kagenti is a security solution that protects multi-agent AI systems through identity-based mechanisms, delegation chains, and open source infrastructure. It addresses data leakage risks in agentic AI deployments by implementing zero-trust principles for agent interactions.
writeup: This video covers Kagenti, a security solution for multi-agent AI systems built on identity-based mechanisms, delegation chains, and open source infrastructure. It matters because agentic deployments introduce data leakage risks when agents interact, and Kagenti applies zero-trust principles to those interactions. The key takeaways: identity anchors agent-to-agent trust, delegation chains govern how authority passes between agents, and the approach targets supply chain security across multi-agent systems.
